item.title: The Story of Peter Keller
item.description:
In April 2012, authorities in Washington state began searching for Peter Keller after his wife and teenage daughter were found dead in their home near North Bend. Investigators said he set fire to the house before fleeing into nearby wilderness.
After days of searching, law enforcement located a hidden underground bunker Keller had spent years building in a remote forested area. Officers surrounded the site and attempted to make contact during a prolonged standoff.
Following the standoff, authorities entered the bunker and found Keller dead inside. Officials concluded he died from a self inflicted gunshot. The case drew attention due to the elaborate bunker and the timeline leading up to the discovery.

media:content.media:title: The Story of Peter Keller
media:content.media:description:
In April 2012, authorities in Washington state began searching for Peter Keller after his wife and teenage daughter were found dead in their home near North Bend. Investigators said he set fire to the house before fleeing into nearby wilderness.
After days of searching, law enforcement located a hidden underground bunker Keller had spent years building in a remote forested area. Officers surrounded the site and attempted to make contact during a prolonged standoff.
Following the standoff, authorities entered the bunker and found Keller dead inside. Officials concluded he died from a self inflicted gunshot. The case drew attention due to the elaborate bunker and the timeline leading up to the discovery.

item.title: He Evaded Capture For 34 Years!
item.description: True Crime Recaps looks back at a case involving a suspect who evaded capture for 34 years.

media:content.media:title: He Evaded Capture For 34 Years!
media:content.media:description: True Crime Recaps looks back at a case involving a suspect who evaded capture for 34 years.
